parse_errors.message: JSON syntax error: trailing comma in array

deeply_nested_json.esm.performance_notes: Should complete parsing within reasonable time limits (< 5 seconds) and memory usage (< 100MB)

large_numbers_overflow.esm.security_notes: Large numbers should be handled gracefully without causing overflow exceptions or infinite values

structural_errors.message: Circular reference detected in expression: y → z → w → y

resource_exhaustion.esm.security_notes: Should handle large files efficiently without excessive memory usage or processing time

null_byte_injection.esm.security_notes: Null bytes and control characters in identifiers should be rejected or sanitized

unicode_attacks.esm.security_notes: Unicode homograph attacks and zero-width characters in identifiers should be detected and rejected

mega_payload_attack.esm.performance_notes: Should handle very large payloads (>100MB) with reasonable memory usage and parsing time limits
mega_payload_attack.esm.security_notes: Memory exhaustion attacks with massive arrays and repeated content should be detected and mitigated

schema_bypass_attempts.esm.security_notes: JavaScript prototype pollution attempts and reserved identifiers should be rejected

excessive_nesting_bomb.esm.security_notes: Deeply nested expressions (>1000 levels) should be rejected to prevent parser bombing

unicode_normalization_attack.esm.security_notes: Unicode normalization forms that create visually identical but different identifiers should be detected and rejected

json_polyglot_attack.esm.security_notes: Polyglot attacks with content interpretable as other formats (HTML, XML, JavaScript) should be sanitized

billion_laughs_json.esm.security_notes: Exponential expansion attacks (billion laughs) should be detected and resource consumption limited
